algorithms.html: The following algorithm generates a witness list for a set of N credential hashes. Inputs: an array of N hashes, in order of their desired witnessIndex values, and the witness length b (default 128). Outputs: a witness seed and encoded witnesses.

steps.html: Generate a UUID v4 value seed using a cryptographically secure random number generator [RFC4122].
Let seedBytes be the 16-byte representation of seed obtained by decoding its hex digits (stripping hyphens) as a big-endian byte sequence.
Let data be a zeroed bit array of N × b bits.
For each integer i from 0 to N-1, inclusive:
Let msg be the ith credential hash from the input array.
Let hash be the output of SHA-256 [FIPS-180-4] applied to the concatenation seedBytes || msg.
Let w be the most significant b bits of hash.
Write w into data at bit offset (i − 1) × b, most-significant-bit first.
Let encodedWitnesses be the multibase-base64url encoding of data, prepended with the prefix character u [MULTIBASE].
Return seed and encodedWitnesses.

algorithms.html: The following algorithm wraps the output of Generate Witness List into a signed verifiable credential.

steps.html: Run Generate Witness List with inputs N and b, producing seed and encodedWitnesses.

Construct an unsigned credential with:
  • type including WitnessListCredential
  • credentialSubject.type set to WitnessList
  • credentialSubject.witnessSeed set to seed
  • credentialSubject.witnessLength set to b
  • credentialSubject.witnessCount set to N
  • credentialSubject.encodedWitnesses set to encodedWitnesses

Sign the credential. If protection against key compromise — including quantum key compromise — is required, the issuer SHOULD use a post-quantum signature scheme and a key pair distinct from those used to sign the protected credentials.
Return the signed credential.

algorithms.html: The following algorithm verifies that a given credential's witness is valid. Inputs: the resolved witness list credential, a credential hash, and the witness index i.

steps.html: Verify all proofs on the witness list credential. If any proof fails, return an error.
Let subject be the witness list credential's credentialSubject.
Let seed be subject.witnessSeed.
Let b be subject.witnessLength.
Let N be subject.witnessCount.
If i < 1 or i > N, return an error indicating the index is out of range.
Let encodedWitnesses be subject.encodedWitnesses.
Verify that encodedWitnesses begins with the character u. If not, return an error.
Let data be the result of base64url-decoding the substring of encodedWitnesses after the first character.
Extract bits at offset (i − 1) × b through i × b − 1 from data, most-significant-bit first. Let this be storedW.
Let seedBytes be the 16-byte representation of seed obtained by decoding its hex digits (stripping hyphens) as a big-endian byte sequence.
Let msg be the input credential hash.
Let hash be SHA-256 [FIPS-180-4] of seedBytes || msg.
Let computedW be the most significant b bits of hash.
If storedW equals computedW, return valid. Otherwise return invalid.
Locate a credentialStatus entry of type WitnessListEntry.
Let the witness list credential URL be the value of the entry's witnessListCredential property.
Dereference that URL to obtain the witness list credential.
Run Verify a Credential Witness using the value of witnessIndex as i.
